title: wallet/client.rs: latest_height and broadcast_tx have no per-RPC timeout
description: LightwalletdClient::latest_height() and broadcast_tx() make gRPC calls with no timeout. The connect() call has a 15-second tokio::time::timeout, but the individual RPC invocations do not. A TCP connection that has stalled (e.g. after a NAT timeout with no keepalive packets) will appear connected but RPCs will block indefinitely. In payment_watcher, this blocks the entire inner scan loop. In payment.rs/tx_builder.rs, a stalled broadcast_tx blocks the wallet task holding wallet_pay_lock. The get_block_range streaming call has a 30-second per-message timeout in the scanner, but latest_height and broadcast_tx do not. Fix: wrap each unary RPC call in tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(15), ...).
